REQ] Expose `ClientFactory.CreateCredential` for direct consumer use\",\"body\":\"### Library name\\n\\nMicrosoft.Extensions.Azure\\n\\n### Please describe the feature.\\n\\nI'd like to be able to construct a `TokenCredential` instance from `IConfiguration` using the same logic that `Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` uses today here:\\n\\nhttps://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src/Internal/ClientFactory.cs#L94-L302\\n\\nThis would enable new scenarios for us, such as being able to create the credentials to access Azure AppConfiguration based on previously configured settings in `IConfiguration`.\\n\\nWe are in a situation currently where we need to support both the `VisualStudioCredential` (for local development) as well as the `ClientSecretCredential` for when running in upper environments (`beta`, `staging` and `production`).\\n\\nI'm aware that using `DefaultAzureCredentials` accomplishes that when paired with environment variables, and this is the setup we have in some of our applications: we set the credential to that, and then add `AZURE_TENANT_ID`, `AZURE_CLIENT_ID` and `AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` environment variables when needed. This works well but only when using environment variables is possible.\\n\\nWe hit a situation now where we'd like to configure both the tenant ID and the client id in `appsettings.json`, and then use those values, if present, to build the required credential to communicate with Azure AppConfig.\\n\\n`Microsoft.Extensions.Azure` has this built-in method that can convert a set of keys from `IConfiguration` into the appropriate `TokenCredential` instance based on the presence of required settings etc. However, this can only be tapped currently when working with Azure Clients and `IServiceCollection`.\\n\\nI opened a related issue last year:\\n- https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/43434\\n\\nBut here I am again, one year later, with a harsher requirement: we are in a position now where using environment variables as a fallback is less desirable.\\n\\nI was told by @jsquire in that issue that the team was already looking into better ways of integrating credentials with `IConfiguration`, but I've not yet seen any movement towards that (unless I'm missing some package, in which case please let me know).\\n\\nWhile the team decides on a stronger, more comprehensive way to better integrate `IConfiguration` with other Azure modules, wouldn't it be possible to just expose that `ClientFactory.CreateCredential` method either as-is, or just extract it into a `public` `TokenFactory` class that is then reused inside `ClientFactory` and can also be used by consumers of the NuGet package.\\n\\nIn the old issue, I said I was trying to avoid creating custom extensions on our side to do this and favored just using environment variables instead, but now with a few new projects this has become more cumbersome to support so I will likely change my stance on that and just copy the code from `ClientFactory.CreateCredential` into a reusable class library on our own side in the meantime.\",\"author_association\":\"NONE\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"julealgon\",\"id\":461579,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjQ2MTU3OQ==\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/461579?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/julealgon\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/julealgon/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1178447860,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Client\",\"name\":\"Client\",\"color\":\"ffeb77\",\"description\":\"This TokenCredential of AddBlobServiceClient(...) is always DefaultAzureCredential\",\"body\":\"### Library name and version\\n\\nMicrosoft.Extensions.Azure 1.11.0\\n\\n### Describe the bug\\n\\nWithin the `BlobClientBuilderExtensions` the method\\n\\n```C#\\npublic static IAzureClientBuilder\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e AddBlobServiceClient\\u003cTBuilder\\u003e(this TBuilder builder, Uri serviceUri, TokenCredential tokenCredential)\\n    where TBuilder : IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithCredential\\n{\\n    return builder.RegisterClientFactory\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e((options, token) =\\u003e new BlobServiceClient(serviceUri, token, options));\\n}\\n```\\nis not passing down the provided `TokenCredential`.\\n\\n### Expected behavior\\n\\nThe provided `TokenCredential` will be used, maybe like this:\\n\\n```C#\\npublic static IAzureClientBuilder\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e AddBlobServiceClient\\u003cTBuilder\\u003e(this TBuilder builder, Uri serviceUri, TokenCredential tokenCredential)\\n    where TBuilder : IAzureClientFactoryBuilderWithCredential\\n{\\n    return builder.RegisterClientFactory\\u003cBlobServiceClient, BlobClientOptions\\u003e((options, _) =\\u003e new BlobServiceClient(serviceUri, tokenCredential, options));\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Actual behavior\\n\\nThe `DefaultAzureCredential` is always being used, which leads to unnecessary token requests, that mostly cannot be resolved and even throw exceptions when some configuration is missing.\\n\\n### Reproduction Steps\\n\\nAdd the service to the `IServiceCollection` like this:\\n\\n```C#\\nservices.AddAzureClients(\\n    config =\\u003e\\n    {\\n        config.AddBlobServiceClient(\\n            new Uri(\\\"\\u003curl to the storage account\\u003e\\\"),\\n            new AzureCliCredential());\\n    });\\n```\\n\\n### Environment\\n\\n_No =\\u003e\\n{\\n     builder.AddServiceBusClient(configuration.GetSection(\\\"ServiceBusConnection\\\");\\n}\\n```\\n\\nWhen resolving the client, it fails.\\n\\nThe issue is that at runtime the configuration section is provided by CommandLineConfigurationProvider and it seems this provider always return a an empty value for the section instead of just being null.\\n\\n![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7221a926-64e5-40dc-94e6-d11c8fa48e35)\\n\\nSo the following code fails because the condition evaluate to configuration is for example provided with env vars or appsettings.json\\n\\nWould it be possible to test that the value is not empty or that section.GetChildren() is empty ?\
